content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes.

A non-empty set SSS of nodes is called good if the following condition holds:


There exists some node xxx in the tree, such that:

For every pair (y,z)(y, z)(y,z) with y‚Č†zy \ne zyÓÄ†=z, and y,z‚ąąSy, z \in Sy,z‚ąąS, it is true that xxx lies on the path from yyy to zzz (possibly x=yx = yx=y or x=zx = zx=z).


Note that xxx may be chosen from outside the set SSS itself.

Count the number of good sets in the tree. Since the answer may be large, find it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN - the number of nodes.
The next N‚ąí1N - 1N‚ąí1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these N‚ąí1N - 1N‚ąí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč, denoting an edge between uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of good sets modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The given set of edges form a tree
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are 777 non-empty sets, and all of them are good.
Test case 2 : The entire tree is not good as a set, however every proper subset is good.
